dashboard.title: ESPHome Device Builder
dashboard.subtitle: Create and make your smart devices and automations
dashboard.adopt_description: Taking control of {name} creates a local ESPHome configuration. From now on you manage updates from the dashboard — vendor-provided firmware updates won't apply automatically.
dashboard.adopt_encryption_hint: Generates a fresh key for the Native API. Recommended; required for Home Assistant integration.
dashboard.adopt_error_generic: Failed to take control of this device
dashboard.no_results_desc: No devices match "{query}". Try a different name or configuration file.
dashboard.no_results_desc_filtered: No devices match the current filters.
dashboard.logs_web_serial_open_failed: Failed to open the serial port.
dashboard.logs_port_reopen_failed: Failed to reopen the serial port for logs ({port}). The device may still be restarting — click Start to reconnect.
dashboard.delete_selected_desc: Are you sure you want to delete {count, plural, one {# device} other {# devices}}? This will remove their configuration files and cannot be undone.
dashboard.delete_single_desc: Are you sure you want to delete "{name}"? This will remove its configuration files and cannot be undone.
dashboard.delete_bulk_success: {count, plural, one {# device deleted} other {# devices deleted}}
dashboard.archive_selected_desc: Archive {count, plural, one {# device} other {# devices}}? Their build files will be removed and you'll need to recompile from scratch when you restore them. You can find archived devices later from the menu (⋮) at the top right of the dashboard under "Archived devices".
dashboard.archive_bulk_success: {count, plural, one {# device archived} other {# devices archived}}
dashboard.action_clean_build_busy: Wait for the current build to finish before cleaning
dashboard.serial_connect_failed: Could not connect to the device: {error}
dashboard.action_rename_offline_desc: "{name}" can't be reached right now, so it can't be reflashed. Rename the config anyway? The device keeps its current name until you reinstall, and since it won't answer to the new name yet, you may need to reinstall using Advanced options and enter its IP address.
dashboard.action_clone_friendly_name_placeholder: Leave blank to derive from hostname
dashboard.action_clone_friendly_name_helper: Shown in Home Assistant. Leaving this blank derives a label from the new hostname so the dashboard list doesn't show two entries with the same label.
dashboard.action_clone_same_name: New hostname must differ from the source
dashboard.action_friendly_name_helper: Shown in Home Assistant and the dashboard list. The hostname stays the same.
dashboard.action_friendly_name_install_after: Install immediately so the device announces the new name
dashboard.action_friendly_name_install_skipped: The new name lands in the YAML now; flash the device to apply.
dashboard.action_api_key_not_found: Unable to automatically extract API key. It may not be set. Open the configuration editor and look for the api: section with an encryption key.
dashboard.logs_method_title: How to get the logs for your device?
dashboard.logs_method_wireless_desc: Requires the device to be online
dashboard.logs_web_serial_unsupported: Web Serial is not supported in this browser
dashboard.logs_serial_disabled: Serial logging is disabled for this device (logger baud_rate is 0).
dashboard.install_method_title: How do you want to install the firmware?
dashboard.install_method_network_desc: Compile and upload over the network
dashboard.install_method_network_desc_offline: Device is offline; compile will run and will trigger update when device is online.
dashboard.install_method_network_address_desc: Target a specific address instead of the auto-detected one
dashboard.install_method_usb_local_desc: For devices connected via USB to this computer
dashboard.install_method_usb_local_unsupported: Requires a browser with Web Serial support (e.g. Chrome, Edge, or Firefox 151+)
dashboard.install_method_usb_local_insecure: Web Serial needs a secure origin (https or localhost)
dashboard.install_method_usb_local_insecure_link: Web Serial needs a secure origin; open the dashboard at {link}
dashboard.install_method_usb_server: Plug into computer running ESPHome Device Builder
dashboard.install_method_usb_server_desc: For devices connected via USB to the server
dashboard.install_method_usb_server_localhost_desc: ESPHome on this computer flashes it over USB
dashboard.install_method_usb_server_ha: Plug into your Home Assistant server
dashboard.install_method_usb_server_ha_desc: For devices connected via USB to your Home Assistant server
dashboard.install_method_web_download_desc: Requires a browser with Web Serial support (e.g. Chrome, Edge, or Firefox 151+)
dashboard.install_method_manual_download_desc: Compile here and download the binary, then flash it manually with your preferred tool
dashboard.install_method_no_ports: No serial ports found. Make sure the device is connected to the server.
dashboard.table_status_unencrypted_tooltip: API traffic is unencrypted — anyone on the LAN can access this device
dashboard.table_status_encryption_pending_tooltip: Encryption is configured in the YAML but the device is still running plaintext — install the firmware to apply
dashboard.table_status_encryption_mismatch_tooltip: YAML enables encryption but the device is broadcasting plaintext — reinstall the firmware to apply
dashboard.pagination_total: {count, plural, one {# row total} other {# rows total}}
dashboard.drawer_show_mdns_txt_records: {count, plural, one {Show # mDNS TXT record} other {Show # mDNS TXT records}}
dashboard.labels_dialog_empty: No labels yet — create one below.
dashboard.labels_bulk_dialog_title: {count, plural, one {Labels for # device} other {Labels for # devices}}
dashboard.labels_bulk_save_failed: {count, plural, one {Could not save labels for # device} other {Could not save labels for # devices}}
dashboard.labels_bulk_saved: {count, plural, one {Labels updated on # device} other {Labels updated on # devices}}
dashboard.labels_bulk_mixed_title: {name}: Some selected devices already have this label
dashboard.labels_bulk_dialog_empty: No labels yet. Create one from any device's drawer to assign it in bulk.
dashboard.labels_delete_confirm: {count, plural, =0 {Are you sure you want to delete "{name}"? It isn't currently assigned to any device.} one {Are you sure you want to delete "{name}"? It will be removed from # device.} other {Are you sure you want to delete "{name}"? It will be removed from # devices.}}
dashboard.filter_menu_active: {count, plural, one {# active filter} other {# active filters}}
dashboard.drawer_auto_loaded_integrations: Show {count, plural, one {# auto-loaded dependency} other {# auto-loaded dependencies}}
dashboard.drawer_config_hash_out_of_sync: Out of sync — flash to update
dashboard.drawer_version_out_of_sync: Out of sync — flash to update
dashboard.queued_update_desc: An offline update has been compiled and is waiting for the device to wake up. Clear this to remove the queued update.
dashboard.queued_update_confirm_desc: This will remove the compiled offline update from the device. The device will need to be reconnected to receive a new update.
dashboard.queued_update_clear_failed_desc: The queued update could not be cleared. The device may still have the compiled update waiting.
dashboard.archive_desc: Archive "{name}"? Its build files will be removed and you'll need to recompile from scratch when you restore it. You can find archived devices later from the menu (⋮) at the top right of the dashboard under "Archived devices".
dashboard.action_archive_success_hint: Restore from the menu (⋮) at the top right under "Archived devices".
dashboard.delete_archived_desc: Are you sure you want to permanently delete the archived device "{name}"? This will remove its YAML and stored configuration. This cannot be undone.
dashboard.archived_dialog_desc: Devices you've archived are kept here so you can restore them later. Use Unarchive to put one back in your active device list, or Delete permanently to remove it for good.
dashboard.archived_dialog_empty: You haven't archived any devices yet.

command.install_no_compatible_peer_offline: Paired build servers are offline — wait for one to reconnect, or relax the policy in Settings → Build server.
command.install_no_compatible_peer_version: No paired build server runs ESPHome {local} — update one, or relax the policy in Settings → Build server.
command.install_no_compatible_peer_mixed: No compatible build server (you're on {local}) — wait for a reconnect, or relax the policy in Settings → Build server.
command.try_reset_suggestion: It looks like you're having trouble with your build. Try {clean_action} first; if that doesn't help, try {reset_action}.
command.try_reset_suggestion_remote: It looks like you're having trouble with your build. Try {clean_action} first; if that doesn't help, ask the operator of {receiver} to reset the build environment from their own dashboard.
command.try_clean_button: cleaning the build files for this device
command.validation_failed_suggestion: Looks like a configuration error. Try {editor_action} to fix the YAML.
command.try_open_editor_button: opening this device in the editor
command.show_secrets_tooltip: Reveal resolved !secret values in the validate output
command.show_logs_after_install_tooltip: Toggle whether to open device logs automatically after a successful install (like esphome run)
command.reset_success: Build environment reset. The next compile will redownload the toolchain.
command.reset_failed: Failed to reset the build environment.
command.queued_message: Another firmware task is running. This task will start when it finishes.
command.queued_waiting_for_build_server: Waiting for another job running on the build server.
command.force_local_failed: Couldn't switch to a local build

firmware.download_type_factory_desc: Full image for ESPHome Web and other flashing tools.
firmware.download_type_ota_desc: For updating a device over the network.
firmware.download_type_elf_desc: For decoding crash backtraces with the ESP stack trace decoder.
firmware.download_type_uf2_desc: Drag-and-drop flashing over USB mass storage.
firmware.flashing_keep_visible: Keep this window visible — switching to another tab or window can interrupt the flash.
firmware.no_flashable_binary: No flashable binary available for this device. web.esphome.io supports ESP32 and ESP8266 only.
firmware.no_binaries: No binaries were produced for this device. Make sure the build completed successfully.
firmware.no_binaries_remote: The build on {receiver} reported success, but no firmware binaries came back to this dashboard.
firmware.no_binaries_remote_detail: The build server may have failed to package or transfer the firmware. Check the build server logs, then try again.
firmware.flash_failed: Failed to flash firmware to device.
firmware.chip_mismatch: Chip mismatch: detected {detected} but device expects {expected}.
firmware.web_download_done_body: Saved as {filename}. Open web.esphome.io on a computer that can connect to your device via USB and flash the file.
firmware.web_download_step_connect: Click Connect and pick your device's serial port
firmware.web_download_step_install: Choose Install and select the downloaded {filename}
firmware.binary_download_done_body: Saved as {filename}. Flash it to your device with your preferred tool.
firmware.elf_download_done_body: Saved as {filename}. Use it with the ESP stack trace decoder to decode crash backtraces.
firmware.choose_binary_desc: Each option explains what it's for.

serial.title: USB Device
serial.no_firmware: No firmware binary found — compile the device first.
serial.connect_failed: Could not connect to the device. Unplug and replug the cable, hold the BOOT button while plugging in, or try another USB port or browser.

wizard.no_boards_found: No boards found. Try a different search.
wizard.dont_know_board: I don't know what board I have
wizard.connect_your_board_select_port_localhost: Connect the board to a USB port of your computer
wizard.connect_your_board_select_port_ha: Connect the board to your Home Assistant server
wizard.connect_your_board_select_port_remote: Connect the board to the computer running the device builder
wizard.connect_your_board_no_ports: No serial ports found. Plug in the board and try again.
wizard.connect_your_board_detect_failed: Could not detect the connected board. Check the cable and try another port.
wizard.how_create: How would you like to create your project?
wizard.create_new_desc: A guided process to get you started with a starter kit or any other ESP boards
wizard.import_file_desc: Use an existing ESPHome configuration (.yaml) or a bundle.
wizard.empty_config_desc: For manually writing or pasting a configuration.
wizard.drop_yaml: You can also drag and drop a .yaml file or a bundle here
wizard.section_name_device_desc: Naming your project will show up in the ESPHome dashboard. Choose a recognisable, descriptive and memorable name. You can use component names or relevant words. You can edit this after you finish this project.
wizard.wifi_required_desc: This board connects over Wi-Fi. Enter your network so the device can come online.
wizard.import_general_error: Failed to import the config file. Please try again.
wizard.import_invalid_filename: The filename "{name}" can't be used as-is — it contains only characters that aren't allowed in a configuration filename. Rename the file to something with letters, digits, hyphens, or underscores and try again.
wizard.import_bundle_conflicts_desc: These files from the bundle already exist in your configuration. Check the ones you want to overwrite; unchecked files are kept as they are.
wizard.import_bundle_secrets_note: Secrets from the bundle are merged into your secrets.yaml; existing values are kept.
wizard.overwrite_device_message: A device named "{name}" already exists. Overwriting replaces its configuration but keeps your labels, comment, and board.
wizard.import_partial_desc: The bundle was imported, but {count, plural, one {# existing file you chose to keep was not replaced} other {# existing files you chose to keep were not replaced}}. The device may still use its previous configuration for those.
wizard.create_general_error: Failed to create the device. Please try again.
wizard.board_load_failed: Couldn't load the board details. Please try again.

device.navigator_desc: Navigate, add and remove options to define your ESP device and its functionality.
device.section_core_desc: Your base functionality to allow your ESP board to run/function.
device.section_components_desc: You can add components after finishing your core configuration e.g. sensors.
device.section_automations_desc: You can set up automations once the core config and at least one component is added.
device.confirm_delete_section: Are you sure you want to delete the '{name}' section?
device.yaml_invalid_message: {count, plural, one {ESPHome reported # error in this configuration. Save anyway, jump to it to fix it, or cancel.} other {ESPHome reported # errors in this configuration. Save anyway, jump to the first one to fix it, or cancel.}}
device.change_board_desc: Pick the board that matches your hardware. These are all built on the same chip as {name}, so they're interchangeable.
device.change_board_unsaved: Save or discard your changes before changing the board
device.welcome_banner_body: A minimal default configuration has been applied. From here you can refine the core settings, add components like sensors or lights, and set up automations. Use the Device navigator on the left or the panels below to get started.
device.step_core_desc: The core configuration components are the base building blocks that let your board run ESPHome. They cover settings such as the device name, Wi-Fi or Ethernet network, OTA updates, the API connection to Home Assistant, and optional add-ons like MQTT. Use the Device navigator on the left to view, edit or add core components.
device.step_components_desc: Components are the building blocks that bring your device to life — sensors, switches, lights, displays, buses, and more. Each one represents a piece of hardware on your board or a virtual entity exposed to Home Assistant. Use the Device navigator on the left to view, edit or add components.
device.step_automations_desc: Automations let your device react to events on its own — turning on a light when a button is pressed, sending a notification when a sensor changes, scheduling tasks, and so on. They run locally on the device. Use the Device navigator on the left to view, edit or add automations.
device.multi_value_empty: No items yet — click + to add one.
device.multi_value_yaml_only: This list contains YAML the visual editor can't model — edit it directly in the YAML pane on the right.
device.value_yaml_only: This value can't be modeled by the visual editor — edit it directly in the YAML pane on the right.
device.value_set_in_yaml: Set to {value} in YAML; edit it in the YAML pane on the right.
device.exclusive_group_conflict: Only one can be set. Choosing one removes the others.
device.registry_list_empty_catalog: No options available for this registry.
device.registry_list_no_applicable_options: No options apply to this section.
device.registry_list_unsupported: This field references a registry the visual editor doesn't recognise — edit it directly in the YAML pane.
device.map_empty: No entries yet — click + to add one.
device.constraint_none_or_all: Set all of these together, or leave them all blank: {keys}
device.constraint_all_or_none: Set these together, or leave them all blank: {keys}
device.substitution_unresolved_hint: Substitutions from packages, included files, or the command line aren't previewed here; they're still resolved when the device is built.
device.secret_picker_aria_missing: Stored secret for {field} is missing; pick another or create it
device.secret_picker_missing_placeholder: Enter a value to create it
device.secret_picker_shared_confirm_message: "{key}" is shared; other devices that use it will get the new value too.
device.pin_input_only: Input only — can't be used as output
device.automation_target_api_action: An action exposed to Home Assistant
device.automation_target_no_components: No components are configured yet — add one first, then come back to wire up an automation.
device.automation_target_no_scripts: No script: blocks declared yet. Add one in the YAML or pick a different target.
device.automation_target_no_lights: No light components configured — add one first to wire effects.
device.automation_target_api_action_new_id_labe: Name for the new API action
device.api_action_header_description: A named action your Home Assistant can invoke. Declare typed `variables:` to accept arguments from the caller, then list the actions that run when it's called.
device.api_action_id_description: The name Home Assistant uses to call this action. Renaming it requires updating any callers that reference the old name.
device.api_action_variables_description: Typed values the caller can pass when invoking this action. Reference them inside actions via `id(name)`.
device.api_action_actions_description: The ordered list of actions to run when this API action is invoked.
device.script_header_description: A reusable list of actions. Once declared, call it from any automation with `script.execute`.
device.script_id_description: The unique id used to invoke this script via `script.execute`. Renaming requires updating any callers that reference the old id.
device.script_mode_description: How concurrent invocations behave. `single` ignores new calls while the script is running; `restart` cancels and restarts; `queued` lines them up; `parallel` runs them concurrently.
device.script_parameters_description: Typed parameters callers can pass to the script. Reference them inside actions via `id(name)`.
device.script_actions_description: The ordered list of actions this script runs when called.
device.automation_header_description: Runs when the trigger fires. Define the list of actions below.
device.automation_actions_description: The ordered list of actions to run when the trigger fires.
device.automation_wizard_pick_target: What should this automation react to?
device.automation_pick_no_targets: No configured components to target — add a component first.
device.automation_trigger_none_available: No triggers available for this target.
device.automation_parse_error: Could not parse this automation. Edit the YAML directly to recover.
device.automation_partial_hydration: {count, plural, one {# automation form could not be loaded. It may render empty.} other {# automation forms could not be loaded. Some forms may render empty.}}
device.automation_yaml_only_fallback: This automation uses a value the editor doesn't recognise yet. Edit the YAML directly.
device.yaml_only_section: This section doesn't have a structured editor — edit it directly in the YAML pane on the right.
device.security_applied: Save and install the device to apply the new credentials.
device.api_encryption_notice: API is enabled without encryption. Anyone on your network can read or control this device.
device.api_encryption_dialog_body: Encryption will be enabled using a unique key stored in secrets.yaml as {key} and referenced from this config.
device.ota_password_notice: OTA updates are enabled without a password. Anyone on your network can upload firmware to this device.
device.ota_password_dialog_body: A strong password will be stored in secrets.yaml as {key} and referenced from this config.
device.web_auth_notice: The web server is enabled without authentication. Anyone on your network can view and control this device.
device.web_auth_dialog_body: A random username (inline) and password will be generated; the password is stored in secrets.yaml as {key} and referenced from this config.
device.web_auth_error: Couldn't generate the web server credentials
device.add_component_hidden_validation_error: Can't add this component: a hidden field has invalid input. Please report the fields listed below — they should be visible.
device.missing_dependencies_body: Configure these first, then come back to add this one:
device.return_to_after_dep_prefix: Adding a dependency. We'll bring you back to
device.unsaved_message: You have unsaved changes in this device. If you leave without saving, they will be lost.

validation.invalid_device_name: Only lowercase letters, digits, hyphens and underscores
validation.device_name_underscore: Underscores work but aren't valid mDNS hostnames — the device may not resolve on RFC-strict networks
validation.device_name_edge_hyphen: Names starting or ending with a hyphen aren't valid mDNS hostnames — the device may not resolve on RFC-strict networks

wifi.title: Set up Wi-Fi credentials
wifi.intro: Your devices use these to join your network. They're stored once in secrets.yaml and shared across every config you create.
wifi.password_placeholder: Leave blank for an open network
wifi.password_too_short: Wi-Fi password must be at least 8 characters

wizard.ack_failed: Saved your choices, but the wizard couldn't be marked as completed — it may reappear next time.

use_case.title: How will you use this?
use_case.intro: This helps tailor the dashboard to what you need.
use_case.devices_desc: Create, configure, and flash ESPHome devices from this dashboard.
use_case.remote_desc: Use this install purely as a remote build server for other dashboards. Device creation stays hidden.

experience.title: Pick your experience
experience.intro: You can change this any time in Settings.
experience.beginner_desc: Keep it simple and guided, the lightest interface.
experience.expert_desc: Unlock the editor diff, navigator search, and YAML search.

layout.update_all_started: {count, plural, one {# device update started} other {# device updates started}}
layout.update_all_no_compatible_peer_offline: Paired build servers are offline — wait for one to reconnect, or relax the policy in Settings → Build server.
layout.update_all_no_compatible_peer_version: No paired build server runs ESPHome {local} — update one, or relax the policy in Settings → Build server.
layout.update_all_no_compatible_peer_mixed: No compatible build server (you're on {local}) — wait for a reconnect, or relax the policy in Settings → Build server.
layout.feedback_menu: Found a bug? Have an idea?
layout.more_options_with_count: More options ({count, plural, one {# firmware task active} other {# firmware tasks active}})

feedback.title: Found a bug? Have an idea?
feedback.description: Help us improve ESPHome Device Builder by reporting issues or sharing your ideas.

command_palette.title: Command palette
command_palette.placeholder: Type a command — or / to search YAML content

yaml_search.no_match_yaml_preview: {count, plural, one {Try YAML search — found # match across your configs} other {Try YAML search — found # matches across your configs}}

settings.title: Settings
settings.remote_compute_only: Only use this for remote compute
settings.remote_compute_only_desc: Hide device creation and use this install purely as a remote build server for other dashboards.
settings.language_desc: Choose the language used across the interface.
settings.language_help: Is your language missing or incomplete?
settings.theme_desc: Choose between light, dark, or follow your system preference.
settings.expert_mode_desc: Enabling Expert Mode will unlock some additional powerful options in the Device Builder for experienced power users of ESPHome.
settings.expert_mode_feature_diff_desc: Compare your unsaved YAML against the last saved version from the editor toolbar.
settings.expert_mode_feature_navigator_desc: Filter the device navigator by component name or ID.
settings.expert_mode_feature_yaml_desc: Search across all your configs from the command palette and the dashboard.
settings.remote_build_enable_desc: Lets other dashboards on your network compile their YAML here. A paired peer can run arbitrary code on this machine; only pair with dashboards you'd give ssh access to.
settings.remote_build_save_failed: Could not save remote build settings; the toggle was reverted.
settings.remote_build_cleanup_ttl_desc: Hours of inactivity before a cached remote build is reclaimed from disk. The receiver keeps the cached build to skip cold rebuilds on subsequent submissions; lower this to free disk sooner, raise it to keep the cache warm longer. 24 hours is a sensible default.
settings.pair_build_server_section_desc: Add a build server outside this LAN — different subnet, VLAN, or container network where mDNS discovery doesn't reach. Same-subnet receivers show up in the list above automatically.
settings.pair_build_server_row_helper: Type the receiver's hostname and port to start a pair request.
settings.pair_build_server_input_desc: Enter the receiver's hostname and peer-link port. The next step shows the receiver's fingerprint so you can verify it before sending the request.
settings.pair_build_server_input_invalid: Hostname is required and port must be between 1 and 65535.
settings.pair_build_server_preview_unreachable: Couldn't reach a build server at {hostname}:{port}. Check the address and that the receiver's peer-link port is open.
settings.pair_build_server_preview_failed: Couldn't preview the receiver's identity. Try again or check the dashboard logs.
settings.pair_build_server_confirm_desc: Compare the fingerprint below to what the receiver shows under Settings → Pairing requests. They must match.
settings.pair_build_server_trust_warning: A paired receiver compiles your YAML and sees your secrets; only pair receivers you trust.
settings.pair_build_server_receiver_label_helper: Shown to you in the paired-receivers list. Local to this dashboard.
settings.pair_build_server_offloader_label_helper: Shown to the receiver's admin in their pairing requests inbox.
settings.pair_build_server_label_required: Both names are required before sending the request.
settings.pair_build_server_pin_changed: The receiver's fingerprint changed since you confirmed it. Go back, re-enter the address, and verify the new fingerprint before pairing.
settings.pair_build_server_no_window: The receiver isn't accepting pair requests right now. Ask the receiver's admin to open their Settings → Pairing requests page, then try again.
settings.pair_build_server_request_unreachable: Couldn't reach the receiver at {hostname}:{port}. Check the address and try again.
settings.pair_build_server_request_failed: Couldn't send the pair request. Try again or check the dashboard logs.
settings.pair_build_server_sent_desc: Open Settings → Pairing requests on the receiver dashboard at {hostname}:{port} and click Accept. The pair will complete once the admin there approves it.
settings.pair_build_server_sent_pin_label: Your fingerprint (the receiver shows this on its Pairing request screen)
settings.pair_build_server_sent_toast: Pair request sent to {hostname}:{port}. Waiting for the receiver's admin to accept.
settings.pair_build_server_rejected_toast: Pair request to {hostname}:{port} was declined or removed.
settings.paired_build_servers_desc: Build servers this dashboard has paired with for compile jobs. Pending rows are waiting for the receiver's admin to accept.
settings.offloader_remote_builds_enabled_desc: When enabled, the dashboard picks a paired remote build server for each install when one is available; turn off to keep every install local while leaving your pairings in place.
settings.offloader_version_match_policy_any_desc: Route to any paired build server regardless of its ESPHome version.
settings.offloader_version_match_policy_release_d: Only route to servers on the same release line (year + month, e.g. 2026.5). Patch differences (2026.5.0 ↔ 2026.5.1) are allowed.
settings.offloader_version_match_policy_exact_des: Only route to servers running the exact same ESPHome version. Fall back to a local build if no server matches.
settings.offloader_version_match_policy_exact_req: Require exact match — fail install if no compatible peer

Like "Require exact version match", but refuses to fall back to a local build when no server is eligible; the install fails until a compatible server is reachable and version-matched or the policy is relaxed. A briefly-disconnected server surfaces the same failure as a version-mismatched one.
settings.offloader_include_local: Include this machine in the build pool
settings.offloader_include_local_desc: When enabled, this dashboard compiles a build itself whenever every paired build server is busy, so local and remote builds run at the same time. A single build still prefers an idle remote server, so the default of offloading from slow hardware is unchanged.
settings.build_offload_pairing_enabled_title: Use this server when auto-routing installs
settings.build_offload_pairing_version_mismatch_p: Build server runs ESPHome {peer}; you're on {local}
settings.build_offload_pairing_version_mismatch_r: Build server runs ESPHome {peer}; you're on {local}. YAML may not compile across this release gap.
settings.remote_build_submit_no_devices: No device configurations to build. Create one first.
settings.remote_build_submit_rejected: The build server rejected the job: {reason}
settings.remote_build_submit_precondition_failed: The pairing isn't ready (peer-link reconnecting, pairing not approved, or receiver offline).
settings.remote_build_submit_unavailable: Couldn't reach the build server. Try again once it's connected.
settings.remote_build_submit_not_found: The build server or configuration couldn't be found.
settings.remote_build_submit_invalid_args: The build server rejected the request: {details}
settings.remote_build_submit_generic_error: Couldn't dispatch the build. Check the dashboard logs for details.
settings.remote_build_cancel_precondition_failed: Couldn't reach the build server to cancel — peer-link is reconnecting or the pairing isn't ready.
settings.remote_build_cancel_not_found: The build server pairing is gone.
settings.remote_build_cancel_generic_error: Couldn't send the cancel. Check the dashboard logs for details.
settings.edit_pairing_endpoint_desc: Update where this dashboard reaches the paired build server. Use this when the receiver moved to a hostname or port your network can reach but mDNS doesn't broadcast.
settings.edit_pairing_endpoint_unavailable: Couldn't reach {host}:{port}. Check the new address and try again.
settings.edit_pairing_endpoint_not_found: The pairing is gone. Close this dialog and re-pair if you still want to send builds here.
settings.edit_pairing_endpoint_invalid_args: The hostname or port wasn't valid: {detail}
settings.edit_pairing_endpoint_generic_error: Couldn't update the endpoint. Check the dashboard logs for details.
settings.unpair_confirm_body: This dashboard will no longer be able to send compile jobs to the paired build server. You can pair again later if you change your mind.
settings.unpair_confirm_body_named: This dashboard will no longer be able to send compile jobs to {label} ({hostname}:{port}). You can pair again later if you change your mind.
settings.offloader_alert_pin_mismatch_desc: The fingerprint at {target} doesn't match what was confirmed when you paired. The receiver's identity rotated, or the host has been replaced. Re-pair to confirm the new fingerprint, or unpair if you don't recognise the change.
settings.offloader_alert_peer_revoked_desc: The receiver at {target} no longer accepts builds from this dashboard. Reach out to the receiver's admin if this was a mistake; otherwise unpair to clear this alert.
settings.reauth_wizard_step1_body: {label} at {target} responded with a different cryptographic identity than the one you confirmed when you paired. Both fingerprints are shown below; they should match if nothing has changed about the receiver.
settings.reauth_wizard_step2_lede: An identity change like this has two very different real-world causes. Read both before you re-pair.
settings.reauth_wizard_step2_benign_title: The receiver admin rotated the identity
settings.reauth_wizard_step2_benign_body: An identity rotation, an OS reinstall, or a server rebuild can all produce a fresh cryptographic key. The receiver admin will see the new fingerprint on their Build server card; re-pairing confirms it.
settings.reauth_wizard_step2_malign_body: A different host on the network is answering on the same address. Re-pairing here would trust the impersonator with your YAML and your secrets. Do not re-pair; talk to the receiver admin first.
settings.reauth_wizard_step3_lede: Before you re-pair {label}, open the receiver's Settings → Build server card and compare the fingerprint it shows for itself with the one below. Only continue if the two match.
settings.reauth_wizard_verified_checkbox: I've confirmed with the receiver admin that this new fingerprint is correct.
settings.reauth_repair_pin_changed: {label}'s identity changed again between the wizard and the re-pair attempt — the receiver responded with a different fingerprint than the one you just verified. Re-open the alert and verify the new fingerprint before retrying.
settings.reauth_repair_unreachable: Couldn't reach {label}. Check that the host is up, then click Try again.
settings.reauth_repair_no_window: {label}'s pairing window isn't open. Ask the receiver admin to open Settings → Pairing requests, then click Try again.
settings.reauth_repair_failed: Couldn't re-pair with {label}. Click Try again to retry.
settings.build_server_card_desc: Other dashboards pair with this one by confirming the identity fingerprint shown here. Read it back to the operator pairing from the other side; it's the only way to detect a man-in-the-middle on first connection.
settings.remote_build_pin_copy_failed: Couldn't copy to the clipboard. Please select and copy the fingerprint manually.
settings.remote_build_identity_load_failed: Could not load this dashboard's identity. Please reopen Settings to retry.
settings.remote_build_rotate_confirm_body: Every paired sender will see a different identity fingerprint on its next connection and will need to be re-paired. Do this if you suspect the keypair was compromised, or if you're rotating as part of a regular hygiene schedule.
settings.remote_build_rotate_success: Identity rotated. Every paired sender needs to be re-paired.
settings.remote_build_rotate_listener_down: Identity rotated, but the receiver listener didn't come back up. Check the dashboard logs.
settings.remote_build_rotate_already_in_progress: Another rotation is already running on this dashboard. Wait for it to finish before retrying.
settings.build_server_pairing_requests_desc: Senders that have asked to pair with this dashboard. Open Review to compare the fingerprint shown there with the one on the sending dashboard, then accept or reject.
settings.build_server_pairing_window_open_failed: Couldn't open the pairing window. Re-enter Settings to retry.
settings.build_server_pairing_window_extend_faile: Couldn't extend the pairing window. Try again.
settings.build_server_paired_senders_desc: Other dashboards approved to send compile jobs to this one.
settings.build_server_peer_approve_already_gone: That request is no longer pending.
settings.build_server_peer_accept_confirm_warning: Accepting will let this remote dashboard run compile jobs on this host. Treat that as full code-execution access.
settings.build_server_peer_accept_confirm_checkli: The fingerprint below should match what the sending dashboard shows out-of-band.
Only accept if you recognise the sender — once paired, they can dispatch builds until you remove them.
settings.build_server_peer_reject_already_gone: That pairing request is no longer pending.
settings.build_server_peer_dashboard_id_desc: Internal pairings-table key. The label and out-of-band fingerprint identify this sender; the ID is shown for support / debugging.
settings.build_server_peer_remove_confirm_body: The sender will no longer be able to send compile jobs to this dashboard. They can re-pair if you change your mind.

secrets.title: Secrets
secrets.desc: Manage sensitive values referenced in your device configurations with !secret
secrets.edit_out_of_sync: That change couldn't be applied; the editor was out of sync and has been refreshed.
secrets.unsaved_message: You have unsaved changes to your secrets. If you leave without saving, they will be lost.
secrets.wipe_message: This clears every secret from secrets.yaml. Device configs that reference these !secret values will fail to build until you add them back. This can't be undone.
secrets.empty: No secrets yet. Add one below or switch to YAML.
secrets.invalid_key: Names may use letters, numbers, dots, dashes and underscores, and can't start with a number.
secrets.duplicate_key: A secret with this name already exists.
secrets.file_header:
# Secrets — store sensitive values here and reference them
# in your device configurations using !secret <key>
#
# Example:
#   wifi_ssid: "your-network-name"
#   wifi_password: "your-password"

auth.description: Sign in to manage your ESPHome devices.
auth.rate_limited: Too many failed attempts. Try again in {seconds}s.
auth.rate_limited_generic: Too many failed attempts. Try again later.

firmware_jobs.title: Firmware tasks
firmware_jobs.empty_desc: Compile, install, or clean jobs you start will show up here.
firmware_jobs.reset_confirm_message: This wipes the toolchain cache and platform/library installs. The next compile will be slow because everything has to re-download. Continue?
